goal: unfurl-modernization: upgrade to the latest vite and electron, move from npm to pnpm, update all libraries in this codebase to latest except MUI, move the UI from MUI to Shadcn, make the codebase follow a modular structure, add vitest and unit tests so you can verify that everything works. Update the AGENTS.md accordingly.
summary: modernize Unfurl: pnpm + latest vite/electron, MUI → Shadcn, modular layout, vitest

tasks.name: modernize-unfurl
tasks.scopedGoal:
Modernize this Electron + React + Vite app end-to-end on a single branch. Do every step below in order and commit logically as you go.

=== 1. Switch from npm to pnpm ===
- Delete `package-lock.json` and add `packageManager: "pnpm@<latest>"` plus an `engines.pnpm` range in `package.json`.
- Add a `pnpm-workspace.yaml` only if needed for a single-package repo (probably not).
- Replace every `npm run X` reference in the repo (scripts, README, `entrypoint.sh`, `test-docker-build.sh`, `docker/Dockerfile.windows`, GitHub workflows under `.github/workflows/*.yaml`) with the `pnpm` equivalent. Make sure the GH workflows install pnpm via `pnpm/action-setup@v4` (or newer) and cache the store.
- Run `pnpm install` and commit the resulting `pnpm-lock.yaml`.

=== 2. Upgrade Vite, Electron, and every other dependency to latest EXCEPT @mui/* and tss-react/@emotion (we are removing those) ===
- Bump `vite`, `@vitejs/plugin-react`, `vite-plugin-electron`, `vite-plugin-electron-renderer`, `vite-plugin-svgr`, `electron`, `electron-builder`, `typescript`, `eslint`, the `@typescript-eslint/*` pair, `eslint-plugin-react-hooks`, `eslint-plugin-react-refresh`, `react`, `react-dom`, `@types/*`, `dagre`, `reactflow` (note: `reactflow` was renamed to `@xyflow/react` — switch to the new package and update imports), `react-hook-form`, `zustand`, `@types/node` to their current latest stable releases.
- Adjust `vite.config.ts` / `tsconfig*.json` / `.eslintrc.*` for any breaking API changes (ESLint 9 flat config if you upgrade to v9 — convert `.eslintrc.cjs` to `eslint.config.js`).
- `vite-plugin-electron` v0.30+ may have moved exports; update imports accordingly. Verify dev (`pnpm dev`), build (`pnpm build`), and packaging (`pnpm build:linux`) still work.
- Do NOT bump `@mui/material`, `@mui/icons-material`, `@emotion/*`, or `tss-react`; they will be removed in step 4.

=== 3. Add Tailwind + shadcn/ui ===
- Install Tailwind v4 (latest), `class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `lucide-react`, `tw-animate-css`, plus the Radix UI peers shadcn pulls in.
- Run `pnpm dlx shadcn@latest init` (Vite + React + TS preset, default theme, base color slate, CSS variables on, alias `@/*`).
- Add a `@/` path alias in `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.node.json`, and `vite.config.ts` (vite `resolve.alias`) pointing at `src`.
- Add `src/index.css` Tailwind directives and dark-mode setup matching the existing dark theme look.
- Generate the shadcn components needed by the UI: at minimum `button`, `card`, `dialog`, `dropdown-menu`, `input`, `label`, `select`, `textarea`, `tooltip`, `badge`, `separator`, `tabs`, `popover`, `scroll-area`, `toast`/`sonner`. Add more as the migration demands.

=== 4. Migrate the UI from MUI to shadcn/ui ===
- Replace every `@mui/material` and `@mui/icons-material` import with a shadcn component or `lucide-react` icon, and every `tss-react/mui` `useStyles` block with Tailwind utility classes (use `cn()` helper).
- Migrated components: `App.tsx`, `components/FileUpload.tsx`, `components/DownloadButton.tsx`, `components/DemoButton.tsx`, `components/DialogViewer.tsx`, `components/NodeEditor.tsx`, `components/NodeMetadataEditor.tsx`, `components/MetadataConfig.tsx`, `components/MetadataConfigForm.tsx`, `components/EverywhereDialog.tsx`, `modals/faqModal.tsx`, `modals/MetadataConfigFormModal.tsx`, `stores/DialogStore.tsx`. Preserve all behavior (file upload flow, demo button konami code, FAQ modal, node editor, dialog modal stack, metadata config form). The keyframe rotator animation in `App.tsx` must be reproduced with Tailwind keyframes or a small inline `<style>` block.
- Once every MUI usage is gone, remove `@mui/material`, `@mui/icons-material`, `@emotion/react`, `@emotion/styled`, `tss-react`, and `@types/styled-components` from `package.json`. Delete `src/theme.ts` (or rewrite as Tailwind tokens). Run `pnpm install` to refresh the lockfile.

=== 5. Reorganize into a modular feature-based structure ===
Target layout (rename, don't keep both):
```
src/
  app/                  # App shell, providers, routing
    App.tsx
    main.tsx
    providers/
  features/
    file-upload/        # FileUpload + its hooks/stores
    dialog-viewer/      # DialogViewer + EverywhereDialog
    node-editor/        # NodeEditor + NodeMetadataEditor
    metadata-config/    # MetadataConfig + MetadataConfigForm + modal
    demo/               # DemoButton + konami logic
    download/           # DownloadButton
    faq/                # faqModal
  shared/
    ui/                 # generated shadcn components live here
    components/         # shared composite components
    hooks/              # useStorage, useKeyboardShortcut
    stores/             # cross-feature zustand stores (JsonData, Node, Dialog)
    lib/                # cn util, convertors (fromTwee, fromMd), constants
    types/              # interfaces (Node, StoryData, Choice, etc.)
  assets/
  styles/
```
- Each feature folder owns a barrel `index.ts` that re-exports its public API.
- Update every import path; no relative `../../` paths crossing feature boundaries (use `@/features/...` or `@/shared/...`).
- Co-locate per-feature stores when they're feature-local; keep `JsonData`, `Node`, `Dialog` stores in `shared/stores` since they're consumed everywhere.
- Move the Electron entry files (`electron/main.ts`, `electron/preload.ts`) into `src/main/` or a top-level `electron/` package with its own `tsconfig`. Update `vite.config.ts` `electron({ main, preload })` paths accordingly. `electron-builder.json5` `files` glob must still resolve.

=== 6. Vitest + unit tests ===
- Add `vitest`, `@vitest/coverage-v8`, `@testing-library/react`, `@testing-library/jest-dom`, `@testing-library/user-event`, `jsdom`. Add a `vitest.config.ts` (or merge with `vite.config.ts` via `test` field) using jsdom env, `setupFiles: ['src/test/setup.ts']` with `@testing-library/jest-dom` import.
- Scripts: `pnpm test` (run once), `pnpm test:watch`, `pnpm test:coverage`. Make `pnpm test` part of the build pipeline.
- Write meaningful unit tests (at least 12+ tests, not placeholders) covering:
  - `shared/lib/convertors/fromTwee.ts` (parse a sample Twee fixture and assert nodes/start/title)
  - `shared/lib/convertors/fromMd.ts` (parse a markdown fixture)
  - `shared/stores/JsonData` zustand store (set/reset)
  - `shared/stores/Node` store
  - `shared/stores/Dialog` store
  - `shared/hooks/useStorage` (localStorage round-trip via jsdom)
  - `shared/hooks/useKeyboardShortcut` (dispatch keyboard events, assert callback)
  - `features/file-upload/FileUpload` (renders, switching file type updates accepted extension)
  - `features/faq/FaqModal` (opens/closes via store state)
  - `features/metadata-config/MetadataConfigForm` (renders form, react-hook-form submit emits expected payload)
  - `features/download/DownloadButton` (creates blob, triggers download)
  - `app/App` (smoke test that it mounts with the providers)
- Use small inline Twee/Markdown fixture strings; do not depend on `public/Lorcan02.1.twee` in unit tests, but you may copy a tiny subset under `src/test/fixtures/`.

=== 7. AGENTS.md ===
Create (or update if present) `AGENTS.md` at the repo root with:
- Stack overview (Vite + React + Electron + Tailwind + shadcn/ui + Zustand + ReactFlow).
- Folder layout summary (the structure from step 5).
- Setup: `pnpm install`.
- Dev: `pnpm dev` (Vite + Electron renderer + main HMR).
- Build: `pnpm build`; platform packages: `pnpm build:linux|mac|windows`.
- Test: `pnpm test`, `pnpm test:watch`, `pnpm test:coverage`. Note that tests must pass before opening a PR.
- Lint: `pnpm lint` (ESLint 9 flat config) — must pass with zero warnings.
- Conventions: feature-based folders, barrel exports, `@/` alias, no inline imports, Tailwind utilities + shadcn primitives, lucide icons.
- Where to add a new feature (create `src/features/<name>/`, expose via `index.ts`, add tests in same folder).

=== Quality bar ===
- `pnpm install`, `pnpm lint`, `pnpm test`, `pnpm build` all pass cleanly at the end. Capture the final output in your handoff.
- `tsc --noEmit` is clean.
- No `@mui/*`, `@emotion/*`, `tss-react`, `reactflow` (old name), or `package-lock.json` remain in the repo.
- The dev server still loads the existing UI (logo, header rotator, FAQ modal, file uploader, metadata config) — at minimum a screenshot or a recorded `npx playwright` or `pnpm dev` smoke confirming the page renders without console errors. UI visuals must match the spirit of the original dark theme (dark background, blue/info accents).
- Push your branch and open a draft PR against `master` with a clear changelog-style description.
acceptance: package-lock.json is deleted; pnpm-lock.yaml is committed; package.json declares packageManager: pnpm@<version>

vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, vite-plugin-electron, vite-plugin-electron-renderer, vite-plugin-svgr, electron, electron-builder, typescript, eslint, react, react-dom, react-hook-form, zustand, dagre, @types/* are at their current latest stable versions
reactflow is replaced by @xyflow/react and all imports updated
No @mui/*, @emotion/*, tss-react, or @types/styled-components remain in package.json or in any src/ import
Tailwind v4 + shadcn/ui are installed; src/shared/ui contains the generated shadcn components used by the app
Every component formerly using MUI now uses shadcn/Tailwind/lucide-react with equivalent behavior
src/ is reorganized into app/, features/<feature>/, shared/{ui,components,hooks,stores,lib,types}, assets/, styles/ with barrel exports and a `@/` alias
vitest is configured with jsdom and Testing Library; pnpm test runs >=12 meaningful unit tests covering convertors, stores, hooks, and key components; all tests pass
pnpm install, pnpm lint, pnpm test, pnpm build all succeed; tsc --noEmit is clean
AGENTS.md at repo root documents stack, layout, install/dev/build/test/lint commands, and contribution conventions
GitHub workflows in .github/workflows/*.yaml install and use pnpm instead of npm
A draft PR is opened against master with a changelog-style description
tasks.verify:
## Setup
- Install pnpm if needed: `corepack enable && corepack prepare pnpm@latest --activate`.
- `pnpm install` at the repo root.

## Automated
- `pnpm lint` → zero warnings.
- `pnpm test` → all tests pass; capture pass count.
- `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit` → no type errors.
- `pnpm build` → renderer + electron main + preload all build.
- Grep checks: `rg "@mui/|tss-react|@emotion|from 'reactflow'" src/` returns empty; `ls package-lock.json` returns not-found; `ls pnpm-lock.yaml` exists.

## Manual
- `pnpm dev` and confirm the renderer page loads at the printed Vite URL (or under Electron) with no red console errors. Verify: logo appears, the Twee/Obsidian/md/JSON rotator animates, the file upload widget is interactive (selecting Twee/JSON/md changes the accepted extension), and the FAQ button opens the FAQ modal. Capture a screenshot or short screen recording and reference it in the handoff.

## Gotchas
- vite-plugin-electron may have renamed exports in v0.30+; check the upgrade notes.
- Tailwind v4 uses `@import "tailwindcss"` rather than the three `@tailwind` directives; the shadcn init wizard will handle this when targeting v4.
- ReactFlow's new `@xyflow/react` package has slightly renamed CSS imports (`@xyflow/react/dist/style.css`).

tasks.name: verify-modernization
tasks.scopedGoal:
Verify the unfurl modernization branch end-to-end against every acceptance criterion of modernize-unfurl. Do not modify src files; you may add throwaway verification scripts to your verifier branch if useful.

Walk through the verifier plan below: install with pnpm, run lint/test/typecheck/build, grep for leftover MUI/tss-react/reactflow imports and package-lock.json, then start the dev server (or Electron) and click through the file upload, file-type selector, FAQ modal, and metadata config flow. Capture at least one screenshot and (when possible) a short screen recording showing the renderer page loaded without console errors. Report results via the standard Verification handoff, picking the strongest accurate value (live-ui-verified if you exercised the UI in a browser/Electron, unit-test-verified if only the test suite ran, type-check-only / verifier-blocked / verifier-failed otherwise).
acceptance: Verification section reports an honest verdict tied to actual command execution evidence
All modernize-unfurl acceptance criteria checked with concrete evidence (command output, grep results, or screenshot/recording artifact paths)
If anything fails, the Findings section enumerates each failure with severity
